name: OmopMedicationAdministrationDrugExposureView
title: MedicationAdministration → drug_exposure (flat)
description: Inpatient medication administration events. Each MedicationAdministration produces one drug_exposure row distinguished by drug_type_concept_id = 38000179 (Physician administered drug). MedicationAdministration carries the richest route and dose-event timing information of the four medication resources.

column.name: drug_exposure_id
column.description: Surrogate key. Generated deterministically from MedicationAdministration.id.

column.name: person_id
column.description: Resolved from subject reference to Patient.

column.name: drug_concept_id
column.description: RxNorm / ATC / NDC mapped to OMOP standard concept. medicationCodeableConcept resolved inline; medicationReference resolved via Medication resource lookup. Placeholder: 0 when concept lookup unavailable.

column.name: drug_exposure_start_date
column.description: Point-in-time (effectiveDateTime) or period start. Resource skipped if neither is available.

column.name: drug_exposure_end_date
column.description: When effectiveDateTime is used (single point), end = start. When effectivePeriod has no end, end = start. omoponfhir general rule: setDrugExposureEndDate(startDate) when no period end. — transform: effectivePeriod.end ?? drug_exposure_start_date

column.name: drug_exposure_end_datetime
column.description: Full ISO datetime. Null if no period end.

column.name: quantity
column.description: Dose amount given. ETL-German computes mean of Range when doseRange is used.

column.name: route_concept_id
column.description: Administration route via SNOMED to OMOP Route domain lookup. MedicationAdministration carries the richest route data of the four medication resources.

column.name: route_source_value
column.description: Raw route text or first coding display.

column.name: visit_occurrence_id
column.description: Resolved from context reference to Encounter.

column.name: drug_source_value
column.description: Best code by vocabulary priority (RxNorm > ATC > NDC > first). ETL-German uses ATC code.

column.name: dose_unit_source_value
column.description: Raw dose unit string from dosage.dose.
